<section id="in-memory-page-methods">

<!-- Start generated docs for Hyde\Pages\InMemoryPage -->
<!-- Generated by HydePHP DocGen script at 2026-07-16 10:34:55 in 0.13ms -->

#### `make()`

Static alias for the constructor.

```php
InMemoryPage::make(string $identifier, FrontMatter|array $matter, string|Closure(static):string|null $contents, string|null $view): static
```

#### `__construct()`

Create a new in-memory/virtual page instance.

The in-memory page class offers three content strategies. You can pass a literal string, or closure to the `$contents` parameter, or pass a view name or Blade file to the `$view` parameter. Closures return strings and are invoked during compile time. We inject the page instance as their first argument, though closures that don&#039;t need the page context can simply declare no parameters.

Contents and views are alternative content sources and cannot be used together. Omit both to create an empty page. Pass null to omit a content source. An empty string is a valid literal for contents, but is not a valid view.

If the identifier for an in-memory page is &quot;foo/bar&quot; the page will be saved to &quot;_site/foo/bar.html&quot;. You can then also use the route helper to get a link to it by using the route key &quot;foo/bar&quot;. Take note that the identifier must be unique to prevent overwriting other pages. all this data will be passed to the view rendering engine.
- **Parameter $identifier:** The identifier of the page. This is used to generate the route key which is used to create the output filename.
- **Parameter $matter:** The front matter of the page. When using the Blade view rendering option,
- **Parameter $contents:** Literal page contents or a closure that lazily generates them. The closure may declare the page parameter or omit it.
- **Parameter $view:** The non-empty view key or Blade file for the view to use to render the page contents, or null when no view is used.


```php
$page = new InMemoryPage(string $identifier, \Hyde\Markdown\Models\FrontMatter|array $matter, string|Closure(static):string|null $contents, string|null $view): void
```

- **Throws:** \InvalidArgumentException If both contents and a view are supplied, or if the view is an empty string.

#### `getContents()`

Get the literal contents or invoke the configured content closure with the current page as its first argument.

```php
$page->getContents(): string
```

#### `getBladeView()`

Get the view key or Blade file for the view to use to render the page contents, or an empty string when the page uses no view.

```php
$page->getBladeView(): string
```

#### `compile()`

Get the contents that will be saved to disk for this page.

The configured content source is selected during construction. Extend this class and override the method for complete control.

```php
$page->compile(): string
```

<!-- End generated docs for Hyde\Pages\InMemoryPage -->

</section>
